description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 threat & injection cluster: system-prompt contents/secrecy-dependence vs injection defense vs user-data disclosure vs credential custody, and against the shipped secrets-identity-hardener.

cases.prompt: Is it a problem that our security relies on the system prompt staying secret, and that the prompt has our internal endpoints in it?
cases.reason: Secrets in the prompt and dependence on prompt secrecy is this skill (LLM07); prompt-injection-defender is the input side and sensitive-disclosure-guard covers user/context data, not the prompt itself.

cases.prompt: Stop untrusted document content from overriding our agent's instructions at runtime.
cases.reason: Preventing untrusted content from changing behavior is prompt-injection-defender; this skill reviews the prompt's contents and whether security wrongly depends on its secrecy.

cases.prompt: Make sure the model doesn't leak PII or another user's data in its responses.
cases.reason: Sensitive user/context/output data leakage is sensitive-disclosure-guard (LLM02); this skill is specifically about the system prompt and secrecy-as-control.

cases.prompt: Rotate the leaked key and set up proper server-side secret storage and least-privilege service accounts.
cases.reason: The custody/rotation mechanics are secrets-identity-hardener; this skill finds the secret in the prompt and routes it there, but doesn't own secret storage design.
